nodes.description: Send/receive emails via Gmail API (OAuth)

credentialId.description: Stored credential reference (optional; OAuth handled via Connections)

recipientSource.description: How recipients are chosen when sending. Manual: type addresses in Recipient emails. Extract from sheet: runtime uses upstream row data first (typically from a Google Sheets node before Gmail). If upstream has no usable emails, optional inline spreadsheet ID + sheet/range on this node fetches via Google Sheets API (same Google account as Gmail). Precedence: upstream wins; inline fetch is only a fallback.

options.label: Extract recipient email(s) from Google Sheets output

contextHints.message: Enter recipients in Recipient emails (comma-separated). Recipient source is only the mode selector—not where you type email addresses.

Precedence: (1) emails from upstream nodes (prefer a Google Sheets node with spreadsheet + range there). (2) If none found, optional inline Spreadsheet ID + sheet/range below fetches rows via Sheets API. (3) Connect Google with Gmail + Sheets scopes.

recipientEmails.description: Recipient email address(es), comma- or newline-separated (e.g. a@x.com, b@y.com). Active when Recipient source is Manual entry. If Extract from sheet is selected, this field is optional — the workflow supplies emails from upstream nodes.

spreadsheetId.description: Optional fallback Spreadsheet ID — active when Recipient source is Extract from sheet and upstream data has no usable recipient rows. Leave empty if a Google Sheets node upstream already supplies rows.

sheetName.description: Sheet tab name for optional inline fallback read (default Sheet1). Ignored unless Spreadsheet ID is set.

range.description: Optional A1 range within the sheet for inline fallback (e.g. A2:D500). Empty reads the whole tab. Same format as the Google Sheets node.

useAiRecipientMapping.description: When enabled, scan every cell in row objects for email addresses (not only columns named like "email"). Use when column headers are messy; still applies after upstream data or inline sheet fetch.

body.description: Email body content (required for send operation)

from.description: Sender email address (optional - uses OAuth account if not provided)

messageId.description: Gmail message ID (required ONLY for get operation, not for send)

requirements.description: Stored credential reference (optional; OAuth handled via Connections)

contextHints.message: Enter recipients in Recipient emails (comma-separated). Recipient source is only the mode selector—not where you type email addresses.

Precedence: (1) emails from upstream nodes (prefer a Google Sheets node with spreadsheet + range there). (2) If none found, optional inline Spreadsheet ID + sheet/range below fetches rows via Sheets API. (3) Connect Google with Gmail + Sheets scopes.

recipientEmails.description: Recipient email address(es), comma- or newline-separated (e.g. a@x.com, b@y.com). Active when Recipient source is Manual entry. If Extract from sheet is selected, this field is optional — the workflow supplies emails from upstream nodes.

sheetName.description: Sheet tab name for optional inline fallback read (default Sheet1). Ignored unless Spreadsheet ID is set.

range.description: Optional A1 range within the sheet for inline fallback (e.g. A2:D500). Empty reads the whole tab. Same format as the Google Sheets node.

useAiRecipientMapping.description: When enabled, scan every cell in row objects for email addresses (not only columns named like "email"). Use when column headers are messy; still applies after upstream data or inline sheet fetch.
